California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Frederick K. Lowell 
Pillsbury, Madison & Sutro 
Post Office Box 7880 
San Francisco, CA 
94120 
Dear Mr. Lowell: 
June 21, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-368 
We have received your letter of June 20, 1989 (copy attached) 
seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided in our 
recent telephone conversation. 
This is to confirm that your let-
ter accurately reflects that ad~ice. 
If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5901. 
KED:JGM:plh 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
By:tn~. :::::a~ 
Counsel, Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322#5660 


LAW OFFICES OF 
PILLSBURY, MADISON & SUTRO 
OTHER OFFICES 
POST OFFICE BOX 7BBO 
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA 
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA 
WASHINGTON, D.C. 
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94120 
TELEPHONE (4IS) 983-1000 
CABLE ADDRESS "EVANS" 
TELEX 34743 
TELECOPIER (4IS) 398-2096 
WRITER'S OFFICE AND 
DIRECT DIAL NUMBER 
235 Montgomery Street 
(415) 983-1585 
John McLean, Esq. 
Senior Commission Counsel 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear John: 
June 20, 1989 
Pursuant to our recent telephone conversations, 
this is a request for technical advice pursuant to Government 
Code section 83114(b). 
This firm is counsel to the Marian Bergeson for 
Lieutenant Governor Committee (the "Committee"), a controlled 
committee established to further the candidacy of State 
Senator Marian Bergeson for the office of Lieutenant Governor 
in the 1990 elections. 
Ms. Bergeson controls another registered 
recipient committee, the Bergeson for Senate Committee (the 
"Old Committee"), which was established to finance her cam-
paigns for the State Senate in prior years. 
The Old Committee 
holds money which was raised prior to January 1, 1989 and, 
in part, prior to June 8, 1988. 
We would appreciate confirmation of your oral 
advice on the following points: 
1. 
In light of the court's order in SEIU v. 
FPPC, Ms. Bergeson may transfer funds from the Old Committee 
to the Committee to the extent that such funds represent 
funds properly raised within the applicable limits of 
Proposition 73. 
The transferred funds may be spent to 
further Ms. Bergeson's candidacy for Lieutenant Governor. 
He understand that it makes no difference if the funds so 
transferred and spent represent monies raised prior to 
June 8, 1988 or were raised during the period June 8 to 
December 31, 1988. 


John McLean, Esq. 
June 20, 1989 
Page Two 
2. 
The 1988 contributors whose contributions 
are transferred from the Old Committee to the Committee 
pursuant to (1) above, may still make additional contri-
butions during the period January 1 through June 30, 1989. 
In other words, in this case, the transfer of contributors' 
1988 contributions does not affect their fiscal year limits 
under Proposition 73. 
3. 
It is not legally necessary for the Committee 
to obtain formal approval from 1988 contributors in order 
to transfer their contributions from the Old Committee to 
the Committee. 
We appreciate your assistance on this matter. 
yours, 
Frederick

sections.question: 1. In light of the court's order in SEIU v. FPPC, Ms. Bergeson may transfer funds from the Old Committee to the Committee to the extent that such funds represent funds properly raised within the applicable limits of Proposition 73. The transferred funds may be spent to further Ms. Bergeson's candidacy for Lieutenant Governor. We understand that it makes no difference if the funds so transferred and spent represent monies raised prior to June 8, 1988 or were raised during the period June 8 to December 31, 1988. 2. The 1988 contributors whose contributions are transferred from the Old Committee to the Committee pursuant to (1) above, may still make additional contributions during the period January 1 through June 30, 1989. In other words, in this case, the transfer of contributors' 1988 contributions does not affect their fiscal year limits under Proposition 73. 3. It is not legally necessary for the Committee to obtain formal approval from 1988 contributors in order to transfer their contributions from the Old Committee to the Committee.
sections.conclusion: We have received your letter of June 20, 1989 (copy attached) seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided in our recent telephone conversation. This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects that advice.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether State Senator Marian Bergeson may transfer funds from her prior State Senate committee to her new Lieutenant Governor committee, and whether such transfers affect contributor limits and require contributor approval under Proposition 73.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirms via written response that the oral advice previously provided accurately reflects the positions raised regarding fund transfers and contributor limits.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (June 20, 1989) and FPPC's response letter (June 21, 1989). The FPPC response is brief, confirming oral advice without restating the full legal conclusions. The numbered questions are extracted from the incoming request portion as they define the legal issues addressed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. In light of the court's order in SEIU v. FPPC, Ms. Bergeson may transfer funds from the Old Committee to the Committee to the extent that such funds represent funds properly raised within the applicable limits of Proposition 73. The transferred funds may be spent to further Ms. Bergeson's candidacy for Lieutenant Governor. We understand that it makes no difference if the funds so transferred and spent represent monies raised prior to June 8, 1988 or were raised during the period June 8 to December 31, 1988. 2. The 1988 contributors whose contributions are transferred from the Old Committee to the Committee pursuant to (1) above, may still make additional contributions during the period January 1 through June 30, 1989. In other words, in this case, the transfer of contributors' 1988 contributions does not affect their fiscal year limits under Proposition 73. 3. It is not legally necessary for the Committee to obtain formal approval from 1988 contributors in order to transfer their contributions from the Old Committee to the Committee.

CONCLUSION: We have received your letter of June 20, 1989 (copy attached) seeking written confirmation of the advice I provided in our recent telephone conversation. This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects that advice.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel confirms in writing the oral advice given to counsel for Marian Bergeson's Lieutenant Governor committee regarding the transfer of funds from her prior State Senate committee, contributor limits, and approval requirements under Proposition 73.
